name: Ragnarok Client Assets Knowledge Pack
description: Knowledge about client-side file packaging (GRF), DATA.INI priorities, SPR and ACT file pairings, and image formats.

entries.title: GRF File Packaging and DATA.INI Priorities
entries.summary: How the client prioritizes file loading from multiple GRFs.
entries.details: Ragnarok Online clients pack files inside custom GRF archives. The loading priority is governed by 'DATA.INI' in the client folder. DATA.INI lists GRFs in descending order of priority: 0 is the highest priority (e.g. '0=custom.grf'), followed by 1, 2, and so on. Assets in higher-priority GRFs will override assets with matching paths in lower-priority GRFs. Loose files in 'data/' directory take the absolute highest priority on legacy clients.
warnings: Writing directly to a GRF file during active gameplay will corrupt the file. RagnaForge operates strictly in dry-run/read-only mode to prevent this.

entries.title: Client Asset Path Normalization
entries.summary: Best practices for locating custom items, NPC and monster sprites.
entries.details: Ragnarok client files are organized into standard directories within the GRF: item inventory icons are under 'data/texture/유저인터페이스/item/', collection illustrations under 'data/texture/유저인터페이스/collection/', equipment sprites under 'data/sprite/아이템/', headgears under 'data/sprite/악세사리/여/' and '/남/', monster sprites under 'data/sprite/monster/', and NPCs under 'data/sprite/npc/'. Path lookup is case-insensitive, but files use EUC-KR encoding (Korean character set Code Page 949).
warnings: Encoding mismatches when naming files inside GRF container (e.g., using UTF-8 instead of EUC-KR) will cause the client to fail sprite detection and display a blank frame or error wrapper.
